pagesMap.summary: IMercati exposes 17 public-facing routes split into 4 layers: marketing/legal pages (/, /aderisci, /privacy, /cookie, /login), province-wide aggregators (/operatori, /calendar), the [marketSlug] zone hierarchy ([marketSlug]/, news, calendar, operators, operators/[id], weather, c/[comuneSlug]), 2 thin redirect-only stubs (/parcheggi, /[marketSlug]/parking), and an operator-self-serve area (/operator, /operator/[id], /operator/[id]/products) that auth-guards via redirect to /login. The codebase shows clear overlap between the province-level /calendar and the zone-level /[marketSlug]/calendar (large copy-pasted FullCalendar setups), and between /operatori (province hub) and /[marketSlug]/operators (zone-scoped list) — same data model, different shells, two style systems. Two pages are entirely vestigial (/parcheggi, /[marketSlug]/parking) — they 1-line redirect because the map is now embedded in the zone page, so they only exist for backward compatibility. One page is broken: /[marketSlug]/search renders hard-coded MOCK operator data from 2024 (Milan coords, Mario/Moda/Libri) and is reachable but not linked from any nav. Styling is bimodal: about half the pages use the curated cream/olive design system, the other half (search, news, login, operator/*) still use generic gray Tailwind cards, suggesting an unfinished design refactor.

routes.purpose: Province-level homepage with hero, MarketsQuickFinder, unified pin map, favorites, manifesto, editorial zone list, and operator CTA.
issues: Inline ZONE_HERO_COMUNE constant duplicated verbatim in /[marketSlug]/page.tsx — should live in lib/.
Pings supabase twice on every render with force-dynamic and no cache headers.
Mixes anchor links (#trova) with Next Link — fine but #trova scroll-mt-24 assumes a fixed nav that doesn't exist in app/layout.tsx (no global header rendered).

routes.purpose: Marketing landing for operator onboarding, hosts the AdesioneForm component.
Static GDPR-style privacy policy.
issues: LAST_UPDATE date '14 maggio 2026' is a hard-coded string — easy to forget on real changes.

routes.purpose: Static cookie policy describing the cookieless analytics and localStorage usage.
issues: Same hard-coded LAST_UPDATE pattern as /privacy — drift risk.

routes.purpose: Email/password Supabase auth (signin + signup toggle), then role-based redirect to operator/admin areas.
issues: Uses generic primary-600/gray Tailwind classes, not the cream/olive design system used elsewhere.
Sign-up flow doesn't redirect after confirmation email — user is left on the same screen with only a text hint.
After login does an extra fetch to /api/operators/me AND a Supabase profile query — two round-trips that could be one server endpoint.

routes.purpose: Province-wide FullCalendar showing all schedule occurrences + events with market/category filter sidebar.
issues: EVT_LABEL, EVT_COLOR, ALL_EVT_CATS, ALL_SCH_CATS and the entire CalItem modal duplicate /[marketSlug]/calendar/page.tsx — ~80% identical code.
No loading skeleton; shows raw 'Caricamento…' text inside calendar slot.
Triple parallel fetch in useEffect with no cancellation — race condition if user navigates away mid-load.
Modal click-outside uses native onClick stopPropagation, no escape-key handler, no focus trap.

routes.purpose: Province-wide hub listing every operator across all zones with filters for market/comune/category and free-text search.
issues: CAT_LABEL map duplicated identically in /[marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx.
Operator links degrade to href='#' when op.market is null — silent dead links instead of disabling the card.
Uses instead of next/image, suppresses lint with eslint-disable — same pattern repeated across operator pages.

routes.purpose: Pure redirect to / (parking map was merged into the home/zone page).
issues: Dead route kept only for backward compatibility — should be a Next.js redirects() rule in next.config.js to avoid bundling a page.
No metadata, no 308 status hint.

routes.purpose: Authenticated operator hub: lists the operator's owned stalls, auto-redirects when exactly one is owned.
issues: Still uses primary-600/gray Tailwind not the cream/olive system.
Triggers a re-fetch + redirect on every mount; if user has 1 operator they always pass through this page briefly (flash of 'Caricamento…').
useEffect missing 'router' from deps.

routes.purpose: Operator self-edit dashboard: name, description, photos, languages, payment methods, social links.
issues: msg state used for both success and error — same gray text style for both; no semantic feedback.
No optimistic UI; user has to wait then read 'Salvato'.
Generic Tailwind palette inconsistent with public pages.
Doesn't render an unsaved-changes warning before route changes.

routes.purpose: CRUD for operator products with photo upload, price, availability toggle, edit modal.
issues: Uses native confirm() for delete — non-styled OS dialog.
Modal lacks focus trap and escape-to-close.
addProduct creates a placeholder ('Nuovo prodotto') row server-side before the user has typed anything — leaves orphan rows if user closes the modal without editing.
Sort_order field saved but no UI to reorder.

routes.purpose: Zone homepage: hero photo, map of all sessions, shortcut grid (operators/calendar/news/weather), comuni cards, schedules list, prev/next zone nav.
issues: ZONE_HERO_COMUNE constant duplicated from app/page.tsx.
Loads ALL markets just to find prev/next index — could be a single ordered query against the current slug.
Layout already calls notFound() if market inactive; this page repeats the same is_active check.
Mixes a flat hero+map row on desktop with a stacked layout on mobile; map height 460 may not match container width when comune list is short.

routes.purpose: Per-zone news/notices feed (market_id OR global) with priority styling.
issues: Still uses the OLD generic Tailwind palette (gray-900, bg-blue-50, bg-red-50) — clashes with cream/olive design used in sibling pages.
No nav back to the zone homepage (no breadcrumb), unlike other [marketSlug] subpages.
OR-clause uses publish_until.gte instead of fetching expired ones separately — items with publish_until=null + expired ones may overlap depending on Supabase OR semantics.

routes.purpose: Zone-scoped FullCalendar showing only this market's occurrences + events with category sidebar.
issues: Heavy duplication with /calendar — same EVT_LABEL/EVT_COLOR/CalItem modal, same FullCalendar config, same upcoming list logic, same toggle helpers. Should factor a shared <UnifiedCalendar /> component.
Fetches all occurrences then client-filters by marketId — wasteful for big provinces.
Doesn't display the market name in document title (only inside body).

routes.purpose: Per-zone search box for operators/categories — intended UX but currently demoware.
issues: CRITICAL: Operators list is hard-coded MOCK data ('Frutti Freschi di Mario', 'Moda & Stile', 'Libri Usati') with Milan coordinates (45.46xx, 9.19xx) — clearly forgotten from initial scaffolding.
Not linked from any nav, header, or [marketSlug]/page.tsx feature grid — a discoverable URL but effectively dead UI.
Generic gray-900/primary-100 palette, not the cream/olive design system.
Result cards link to /[slug]/operators?operator=ID but the operators page doesn't read that query param.

routes.purpose: Weather for a comune within the zone with hourly+daily forecast, severe-weather notifications via NotificationProvider.
issues: First render flashes 'Caricamento meteo…' as plain text instead of skeleton.
useEffect dep is currentComune?.comune string only — if lat/lng change for the same comune name it won't refetch (unlikely but a stale-data bug).
30-minute setInterval keeps running even when tab is hidden — battery drain.
No fallback when /api/weather fails (just sets weather=null and prints 'Dati meteo non disponibili').

routes.purpose: Pure redirect to /[marketSlug] (parking layer was merged into the zone map).
issues: Dead route — should become a next.config.js redirect rule.
Mirrors /parcheggi pattern, so two places to remember when refactoring.

routes.purpose: Zone-scoped operator list with category/search/favorites filters and an interactive 'pin to map' feature.
issues: CAT_LABEL duplicated from /operatori.
Two separate useEffects fetching market info and operators — could be a single endpoint.
pinAllVisible() resets pinned to only currently-filtered ops — if user clears filters afterwards, prior pins are lost without warning.
Doesn't pre-load any pins — map area is empty until user interacts, which can feel like a broken feature.

routes.purpose: Public operator detail page: photos, sessions (where/when), languages, payments, socials, products.
issues: Loads operator and validates market slug client-side via .markets!inner — if slug case differs from URL, falls through to notFound() even for valid operators.
Photos shown via in horizontal scroll without next/image — no responsive sizing.
No JSON-LD/structured data — public detail page that would benefit from SEO markup.

routes.purpose: Comune detail within a zone: photo hero, sessions explorer, operator subset, prev/next comune nav.
issues: Fetches all schedules for the market then filters by slugifyName client-side — should query by a normalized comune_slug column.
Loads all operators of the market without filtering by schedule_id — wastes payload for large zones.
If operator.schedule_id is null, the row still ships down the wire even though ComuneSessionsExplorer presumably filters it out.

redundantPaths: /calendar  vs  /[marketSlug]/calendar  — same FullCalendar component, same color maps, same modal, same upcoming-list logic; only the data scope (province-wide vs marketId-filtered) differs. ~80% duplicated code; should be one <UnifiedCalendar scope={...} /> consumed by both routes.

/operatori  vs  /[marketSlug]/operators  — both render a card grid of operators with category/search filters; province-level just lacks the 'pin to map' affordance and adds a comune filter. Could merge into one parametric page with an optional marketSlug filter.
/parcheggi  and  /[marketSlug]/parking  — two redirect-only files. Both should be removed and replaced by redirects() in next.config.js so they don't ship as React pages.

/[marketSlug]/search  — overlaps with /[marketSlug]/operators (which already has a search input). The dedicated /search page would only make sense if it included products, but products search is explicitly deferred ('Fase 2') and the operators search query also forwards to /operators?operator=ID. Recommend deleting the page until products search lands.

/operator  hub  vs  immediate redirect to /operator/[id] when only one operator exists  — the auto-redirect produces a brief 'Caricamento…' flash on every login for single-stall operators; either redirect server-side in middleware or skip the hub entirely for single-owner accounts.

components.name: AdesioneForm
components.purpose: Client form for operators to send a membership/join request (POST /api/adesioni) with status states idle/sending/success/error and a honeypot field.

components.name: ComuneSessionsExplorer
components.purpose: Interactive viewer for a comune's market sessions: lists sessions per market, classifies schedules, renders the MarketViewer map and operator list, with query-string driven selection.

components.name: CookieNotice
components.purpose: Non-blocking dismissible privacy banner persisted via localStorage; informs about no tracking cookies and Supabase admin cookies.

components.name: DaySelector
components.purpose: Multi-select day-of-week toggle buttons (Lun..Dom) used in admin market editors. Also re-exports formatMarketDays helper.

components.name: ExcelOperatorsTools
components.purpose: Admin toolbar for Excel export/template/import of operators with dry-run preview, error reporting and confirm step; scoped to a market or global.

components.name: FavoriteButton
components.purpose: Star toggle button for adding/removing a market or operator from favorites via the useFavorites hook.

components.name: FavoritesSection
components.purpose: Homepage section that resolves favorite market/operator IDs to display names via API and renders them as links; hidden when empty.

components.name: LocationFields
components.purpose: Reusable form fields combining numeric lat/lng inputs, a Geolocation API 'Sono qui' button, and an optional dynamically-loaded LocationPicker map.

components.name: LocationPicker
components.purpose: Leaflet map with click-to-place + draggable marker for picking a single coordinate; optionally renders a polygon area overlay.

components.name: MarketAreaDrawer
components.purpose: Leaflet+Geoman map for drawing/editing a single market-area polygon (GeoJSON Feature<Polygon>); used in admin place area editor.

components.name: MarketViewer
components.purpose: Thin wrapper that renders UnifiedMapClient with showParkingNearby=true and a fixed default height. Replaces the old Google-Maps based market viewer.

components.name: MarketsQuickFinder
components.purpose: Homepage search/filter widget over market session rows with text search + day filter (today/weekend/specific weekday).

components.name: NavMenu
components.purpose: Slide-out navigation drawer listing all markets and their comuni, with search, role-aware admin links and auth login link.

components.name: Navigation
components.purpose: Sticky top navigation bar: logo, menu button (opens NavMenu), weather widget, admin shortcut and auth state via Supabase.

components.name: NotificationProvider
components.purpose: React context provider wrapping the Web Notifications API (requestPermission, sendNotification, current permission) with useNotifications hook.

components.name: OperatorCard
components.purpose: Card UI for an operator (stall number, rating, languages, payment methods, social links, navigate-to-stall button). Currently UNUSED — not imported anywhere.

components.name: OperatorFilters
components.purpose: Search input + category buttons (all/food/clothing/…) used to filter operators on the per-market operators listing.

components.name: PhotoUploader
components.purpose: Multi-file image uploader to a Supabase storage bucket (operator-photos or product-photos), returning public URLs and supporting removal.

components.name: Providers
components.purpose: Root client wrapper that mounts NotificationProvider and the Navigation bar around app children. Used by app/layout.tsx.

components.name: Reveal
components.purpose: Wrapper that applies a fade-up-on-scroll animation using the useReveal hook, with optional element tag and stagger delay.

components.name: UnifiedMap
components.purpose: Core react-leaflet map handling market/parking/operator pins, custom div icons, polygon overlays, nearby-parking fetch (/api/parking), fitBounds and CARTO Voyager tiles. Exports UnifiedMapPin type.

components.name: UnifiedMapClient
components.purpose: Tiny next/dynamic ssr:false wrapper around UnifiedMap with a 'Caricamento mappa…' loading placeholder.

components.name: WeatherWidget
components.purpose: Compact weather widget for the current market: fetches current/hourly/daily forecast + alerts via API, maps conditions to lucide icons, renders inside the top navbar.

components.name: ZoneImage
components.purpose: Image component that fetches a representative image for a comune via /api/comune-image with fallback query and a Liguria-themed SVG placeholder; optional hover zoom.

components.name: decorations (OliveSprig, WaveDivider, MountainSea, DoubleRule)
components.purpose: Set of decorative inline-SVG components (olive sprig, wave divider, mountain+sea silhouette, double rule line) used as visual accents across the editorial UI.

components.name: admin/AdesioneStatoToggle
components.purpose: Admin <select> control that PATCHes /api/admin/adesioni/[id] to change the state of a membership request (nuovo/in_contatto/aderito/scartato).

components.name: analytics/PageviewTracker
components.purpose: Side-effect-only component that POSTs a cookieless pageview event to /api/analytics/track on mount (type: market/operator/comune/homepage).

duplicates: OperatorCard.tsx vs the inline operator card markup in app/[marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx — same responsibility (render one operator: name/description/category/stall/social/payments/navigate). OperatorCard is currently DEAD CODE (no importers).

MarketViewer.tsx vs UnifiedMapClient.tsx — MarketViewer is a 23-line wrapper that just calls UnifiedMapClient with showParkingNearby=true and a default mapHeight; both [marketSlug]/page.tsx and ComuneSessionsExplorer go through MarketViewer while app/page.tsx and [marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx use UnifiedMapClient directly, so the same 'map with parking pins' use case has two entry points.

LocationPicker.tsx vs MarketAreaDrawer.tsx — partial overlap: both are Leaflet maps that capture geometry. LocationPicker handles single point + optional read-only polygon; MarketAreaDrawer handles polygon draw/edit. Not a strict duplicate but they re-implement very similar Leaflet/icon boilerplate and could share a base map config.

Navigation.tsx + NavMenu.tsx — not duplicates per se but tightly coupled: both independently call supabase.auth.getUser() and fetch the profile.role; the role-loading logic is duplicated across the two.
componentsMap.summary: Mapped all 27 .tsx files under mercati-v2/components/. The codebase has 25 single-purpose components plus the decorations.tsx barrel of 4 SVG decorations and one admin/, one analytics/ component. The architecture is cleanly Next.js App Router: app/* pages import directly from @/components/*. Most components are leaf UI (FavoriteButton, Reveal, ZoneImage, DaySelector, CookieNotice, decorations) with a handful of larger interactive screens (UnifiedMap, NavMenu, ComuneSessionsExplorer, WeatherWidget, ExcelOperatorsTools, MarketAreaDrawer). The map subsystem is layered (UnifiedMap → UnifiedMapClient → MarketViewer) and a single non-trivial dead-code duplicate exists: OperatorCard.tsx is never imported and is functionally replaced by an inline render in app/[marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx. MarketViewer is a thin convenience alias of UnifiedMapClient (showParkingNearby=true). LocationPicker is used only by LocationFields. Role/auth logic is duplicated between Navigation and NavMenu.

apisMap.summary: Mapped 34 route.ts files in mercati-v2/app/api. Endpoints fall into three groups: public read-only data (parking/weather/news/events/markets/operators/schedules/places/products/comune-image), admin-write operations (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE on markets, operators, news, events, products, schedules, places, admins, adesioni, admin/sessions — protected via Supabase RLS + checks like `auth.getUser()` and `market_admins`/`super_admin` lookups, not middleware), and operator/citizen flows (adesioni form, analytics tracker, operator self-service via operators/me, operators/[id]/invite). Three endpoints appear orphaned: `/api/parking/nearby`, `/api/parking/osm`, and `/api/schedules/[id]/area` (the latter is replaced by `/api/places/[id]/area`). All other routes have at least one client caller in `app/` or `components/`. Note: middleware (`middleware.ts` + `lib/supabase/middleware.ts`) protects `/admin/*` pages with super_admin role, but does NOT gate `/api/*` paths — admin-only API enforcement is done in-route (RLS + explicit profile/market_admins checks).

adminMap.summary: IMercati v2 has two admin surfaces: a super-admin area under `app/admin/**` (4 pages + 2 API routes) and a per-zone admin area under `app/[marketSlug]/admin/**` (6 pages). The super-admin dashboard centralizes stats, on/off toggles (zones cascade to sessions), Excel import/export, and the form-submission inbox (adesioni). Per-zone admin handles operators (with cross-market linking, M:N presences, and account invites), news (with global option for super-admin), events, drawing the polygon area per place, and per-session operator configuration. Only two components are exclusive to admin: `ExcelOperatorsTools` (Excel import/export) and `components/admin/AdesioneStatoToggle`. `MarketAreaDrawer` is referenced only from the admin places/area page. Shared form components (LocationFields, DaySelector, PhotoUploader, LocationPicker) are used by both admin and the operator dashboard.

features.name: Super-admin dashboard (stats, KPI, top zones/operators, recent adesioni)
features.description: Server-rendered dashboard aggregating counts (markets, sessions, operators, 7d views, new adesioni) plus tables of top markets/operators (last 30d) from market_stats_30d / operator_stats_30d views, and the 5 most recent adesioni.

features.name: Toggle on/off zones and sessions (Accendi/Spegni with cascade)
features.description: Filterable list of all sessions grouped by zone, with per-session and per-zone on/off toggle. Switching a zone cascades to all its sessions; hidden items disappear from calendar/map/search.

features.name: Super-admin sessions API (all sessions including inactive)
features.description: GET endpoint returning ALL market_schedules (including is_active=false) for the toggle dashboard. Distinct from the public /api/schedules/list.

features.name: Gestione zone (CRUD markets, list + new + Excel tools)
features.description: List of markets with create form (slug, name, city, center coordinates, default zoom, market_days, active flag) and embedded global ExcelOperatorsTools (super-admin import/export across all zones).

features.name: Market edit + market admin assignment
features.description: Edit market fields (slug, name, city, market_days, coords, zoom, active, description), delete market, and manage market admins (invite by email, remove). Calls /api/markets/[id]/admins.

features.name: Adesioni operatori inbox (super-admin)
features.description: Lists submissions from the public /aderisci form with stato filter (nuovo/in_contatto/aderito/scartato), shows email warning if notification failed, embeds AdesioneStatoToggle for inline status changes.

features.name: Adesione status PATCH/DELETE API
features.description: PATCH (update stato or note_admin, with allow-list) and DELETE for adesioni_operatori rows. Used by AdesioneStatoToggle.

features.name: AdesioneStatoToggle component (admin-only)
features.description: Inline select for changing an adesione stato, calls /api/admin/adesioni/[id]. Only used by app/admin/adesioni/page.tsx.

features.name: Per-zone admin home (gestione zona + places + sessions overview)
features.description: Server-rendered hub per zone: shortcut cards (operators / news / events), and per-place breakdown of sessions with counts of bound operators, links to draw area per place and configure each session.

features.name: Per-zone operators list + Excel tools + create (with schedule binding)
features.description: Lists all operators of the zone, embeds ExcelOperatorsTools scoped to marketSlug, and provides a create form with optional schedule binding and LocationFields (map picker).

features.name: Per-zone operator edit (profile, M:N presences, account invite)
features.description: Edit operator profile (name, category, code, stall, description), manage M:N presences across sessions (add/remove/edit position per session via LocationFields), and invite the operator user account via email.

features.name: Per-zone news/avvisi (with global flag for super-admin)
features.description: CRUD for news_items with title, content, type (schedule/notice/event/emergency), priority, publish_from/until window, and is_global checkbox (gated to super_admin role) to broadcast across all markets.

features.name: Per-zone events CRUD
features.description: CRUD for market events with title, description, category (market/fair/food/music/art/sport/other), location, start_at/end_at, and free-text recurrence_rule.

features.name: Place area drawer (polygon per luogo, shared across sessions)
features.description: Loads/saves polygon_geojson for a market_place via /api/places/[id]/area, lists which session days share the area, uses MarketAreaDrawer component.

features.name: Schedule area redirect (legacy → place area)
features.description: Server redirect from schedule-level area URL to the place-level area page (or back to session if no place). Compatibility shim — could be inlined but keeps old URLs working.

features.name: Per-session admin (banchi della sessione: create, link existing cross-market)
features.description: Manage operators bound to a single market_schedule: create new banco with LocationFields, link existing operator (cross-market search via /api/operators/search), shows shared-area info from place, deep-link to draw place area.

features.name: ExcelOperatorsTools component (export, template, import with dry-run preview)
features.description: Used in super-admin /admin/markets (global, all=1) and per-zone /[marketSlug]/admin/operators (scoped). Calls /api/operators/export, /api/operators/template, /api/operators/import (with dryRun preview showing willCreate/willUpdate/errors before commit).

features.name: MarketAreaDrawer component (admin-only polygon editor)

palette.source: tailwind.config.js (NOT tailwind.config.ts — that file does not exist; theme is declared in the .js config). Label in code: 'Palette Ligure'.
palette.usage: Cream = page background + cards + dividers. Ink trio = text hierarchy (DEFAULT/soft/muted). Olive = brand accent for headings italic, CTAs, hover, OliveSprig, map polygons category 'alimentare'. Sea = wave divider + map polygons 'artigianato'. Terra = error state in AdesioneForm + map polygons 'antiquariato'.
palette.coherence: Strong on public-facing pages (home, marketSlug, aderisci, MarketsQuickFinder, AdesioneForm). The body bg #F7F2E7 + body color #1F2813 are hard-coded in globals.css, reinforcing palette discipline. Leaflet tiles are filtered (saturate 0.78, sepia 0.06) to harmonize OSM with cream/olive — a thoughtful touch.
palette.breaks: Admin and operator areas (app/operator/page.tsx, app/admin/markets/page.tsx, app/admin/markets/[id]/page.tsx, app/[marketSlug]/admin/*) use raw Tailwind gray-100/200/500/600/700/900 and red-600 instead of cream/ink/terra. The [marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx mixes brand tokens with amber-100/300/500/800 for 'preferiti' and red-50/200/700/800 for errors — off-palette. 271 occurrences of generic Tailwind palette across 23 files indicate the brand system is enforced only on the marketing/visitor surface.
palette.legacyShim: Retains a 'primary' scale (50..900) for retrocompat — used in admin pages (focus:ring-primary-500, hover:text-primary-600). Functional but conceptually duplicates olive/ink and dilutes the named palette.

usage.font-serif: Display + section headings (h1/h2/h3), italic for poetic accents (e.g. 'terra e di mare', 'IMercati', 'Entrate nel progetto.'), brand mark in footer, FullCalendar toolbar title, market card titles.
usage.font-sans: Body, labels, UI chrome, leaflet popups & attribution (hardcoded 'Inter' literal in globals.css).

typography.scale: h1 5xl→7xl with leading-[1.02]/[1.04] and tracking-tight — strong editorial display. Eyebrows use uppercase text-[0.72rem] / text-xs with custom letterSpacing 'tracking-widest-plus' (0.22em). Tabular-nums applied to numerals (counts, times) — a subtle but consistent typographic detail.
typography.consistency: Excellent on the public surface: every section opens with an uppercase eyebrow + serif H2, often with OliveSprig and a thin olive rule below. The pattern (eyebrow → serif title → italic olive accent → sprig+rule) repeats verbatim across home, market page, and aderisci — visually distinctive.
typography.breaks: Admin pages drop the serif and use Tailwind defaults (text-3xl font-bold text-gray-900) — they look like a different product. Leaflet popup/control CSS hardcodes literal 'Inter' instead of var(--font-sans), so a future font swap at layout level would not propagate to the map chrome.

voice.tone: Intimate, editorial, place-rooted Italian. Anti-corporate, anti-buzzword. Uses 'voi' (formal/respectful 2nd person plural) when addressing operators — a deliberate, almost old-shop register. Short declarative lines with poetic pauses. Avoids 'piattaforma', 'soluzione', 'AI', 'innovativo'.
voice.register: Italian, formal-plural with operators ('Avete un banco', 'Entrate nel progetto', 'Vi accompagniamo', 'Raccontateci di voi'), neutral 3rd-person when describing the project. Cadence is editorial: clauses separated by em-dashes and periods, never exclamation marks. Numbers are concrete promises ('Risposta entro 48 ore', 'Cinque minuti').
examples: Mercati di terra e di mare.
IMercati raccoglie in un unico posto i mercati settimanali della provincia di Imperia. Dove sono, quando, chi c'è. Per i cittadini, per i turisti, e per gli operatori che li fanno vivere.
Un volto per chi sta dietro al banco. Ogni operatore ha la sua scheda: foto, storia, prodotti, dove e quando lo trovate. Diventano persone, non solo banchi.
Un progetto indipendente, fatto sul territorio. Stiamo accompagnando i primi operatori a costruire la propria identità online. Senza promesse miracolose — un passo alla volta.
Avete un banco al mercato? Entrate nel progetto.
Stiamo cercando i primi operatori della provincia. Vi accompagniamo nei primi mesi a costruire la vostra presenza online, gratis. In cambio, vi chiediamo tempo e qualche storia da raccontare.
Cinque minuti. Le risposte non hanno bisogno di essere perfette.
Raccontateci qualcosa di voi — Da quanto lavorate, cosa vi rende diversi, cosa vi aspettate dal progetto…
Grazie. Vi rispondiamo entro 48 ore all'email che avete indicato. Nel frattempo, esplorate il sito: vi può dare un'idea di cosa costruiremo insieme.
Le aree evidenziate sono i perimetri dei mercati. Tocca un pin per i dettagli.
inconsistencies: Admin page copy: 'Non risulta nessuna scheda operatore associata al tuo account. Chiedi all'amministratore del mercato di invitarti.' — switches to bureaucratic 'tu' + administrative tone; collides with the 'voi' editorial register established everywhere else.
Quick finder utility strings ('Tutti', 'Weekend', 'Affina la ricerca o usa i filtri.') are functional but flat — the surrounding manifesto sets a higher bar; opportunity to keep micro-copy in voice.
Footer line 'Dati aggiornati in tempo reale.' is borderline buzzword in an otherwise anti-buzzword voice. 'Aggiornati ogni giorno' would stay in tone.

Mixed punctuation conventions: typographic ellipsis '…' appears in placeholders next to straight ASCII quotes ('Es. "Sanremo", "Mercoledì", "Piazza Goito"…') — Italian guillemets «» or curly quotes would match the editorial register.
Apostrophes are correctly typeset via ' in most places ('c'è', 'Cos'è', 'd'idea') but the pattern relies on per-author discipline; a lint rule would lock it.

illustrations.name: OliveSprig
illustrations.shape: Horizontal stem with 5 leaves alternating above/below — Mediterranean/Ligurian olive branch reduced to a 64x24 viewBox.
illustrations.usage: Hero eyebrow (home + aderisci), repeated decoratively in the 'Cos'è IMercati' block and 'Le zone' block paired with a thin olive rule, footer brand mark. Always text-olive-500 at w-8 to w-10. Used 4+ times in app/page.tsx and once in app/aderisci/page.tsx — a clear leitmotif.

illustrations.name: MountainSea
illustrations.shape: Jagged mountain ridge filled at 8% opacity + outline stroke + a single wave line below. 200x60 viewBox. Encapsulates Liguria's geography (entroterra + mare) in one mark.
illustrations.usage: Only on homepage hero, absolutely positioned top-right, w-[620px], opacity-60, hidden md:block, animate-float-soft. NOT reused on the [marketSlug] hero — which is the natural place to repeat it (each zone is also coast vs entroterra) — nor on aderisci.
illustrations.verdict: Strong mark, under-used. Missed opportunity on zone pages.

illustrations.name: WaveDivider
illustrations.usage: Used as a section title accessory: home 'Dove sono i mercati' and [marketSlug] 'I comuni della zona'. Always text-sea-500 opacity-60, hidden md:block.

illustrations.name: DoubleRule
illustrations.shape: Two thin cream-300 horizontal lines stacked 2px apart.
illustrations.usage: Defined but I did not find it consumed on the audited pages.

illustrations.name: System-level note
illustrations.shape: Three semantic marks: land (olive), territory (mountain+sea), water (wave).
illustrations.usage: Used sparingly enough to feel intentional rather than decorative noise.
illustrations.verdict: The mark system has the right idea (terra+mare); execution is uneven because MountainSea appears only once and DoubleRule never appears.

weakSpots: Admin surfaces (app/admin/markets/*, app/operator/*, app/[marketSlug]/admin/*) use raw Tailwind gray + red palette and lose the serif. They feel like a generic CRUD app bolted onto an editorial site. Grep counted 271 off-palette utility uses across 23 files. This is the single biggest break in brand identity.

Voice register switches inside admin/operator flows: shifts from formal 'voi' to clipped 'tu' or to flat administrative phrasing ('Non risulta nessuna scheda…') — breaks the intimate, place-rooted tone established on the marketing pages.

Legacy 'primary' color scale in tailwind.config.js is still referenced by admin pages (focus:ring-primary-500, hover:text-primary-600). It duplicates olive/ink semantically and signals an incomplete migration from the old palette to 'Palette Ligure'.

Error state styling is inconsistent: AdesioneForm uses bg-terra/10 border-terra/30 text-terra (brand-aligned), but [marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx uses bg-red-50 border-red-200 text-red-800 (generic). Same semantic, two visual languages.
Favorites: [marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx uses amber-100/300/500/800 for the 'solo preferiti' chip — amber is not in the palette. A muted olive or terra accent would stay in family.
tailwind.config.ts referenced in the audit prompt does not exist — the project uses tailwind.config.js. Minor, but suggests the team has not standardized config tooling.

MountainSea illustration appears only on the homepage hero. The [marketSlug] page hero and the aderisci hero use only OliveSprig — they could carry a zone-specific atmospheric mark (mountain vs sea variant) to reinforce the 'terra e di mare' tagline at every entry point.
DoubleRule decoration is defined but unused in the audited surfaces — orphan asset.

Leaflet popup and zoom-control CSS hardcodes the literal string 'Inter' instead of var(--font-sans). A future font swap would skip these surfaces and create a visible inconsistency on the map (which is brand-critical territory since the map is described in code as 'identità IMercati').
Footer microcopy 'Dati aggiornati in tempo reale' is a mild buzzword inside an otherwise carefully anti-buzzword voice — easy fix ('Dati aggiornati ogni giorno').

MarketsQuickFinder placeholder mixes straight quotes with typographic ellipsis: 'Es. "Sanremo", "Mercoledì", "Piazza Goito"…' — Italian guillemets «» or curly quotes would match the editorial register elsewhere.

ZoneImage cards (Wikipedia photos) are not stylistically controlled — they can read as generic stock thumbnails and dilute the bespoke editorial feel established by Fraunces + OliveSprig. A subtle duotone/sepia filter consistent with the leaflet tile filter (saturate 0.78, sepia 0.06) would tie them into the system.
brandAudit.summary: IMercati has a genuinely distinctive, well-considered brand identity on its public surface: a 'Palette Ligure' of cream/ink/olive with sea+terra accents (all hex-locked in tailwind.config.js and reinforced in globals.css body and leaflet filters), a Fraunces+Inter pairing applied with editorial restraint (uppercase 0.22em eyebrows, italic olive accents, serif headings, tabular numerals), three semantically meaningful SVG marks (OliveSprig=land, MountainSea=territory, WaveDivider=water), and a remarkably consistent intimate, formal-'voi', place-rooted Italian voice that actively refuses tech buzzwords ('Senza promesse miracolose — un passo alla volta', 'Diventano persone, non solo banchi'). The system breaks down sharply once you leave the marketing/visitor pages: admin/operator/CRUD surfaces revert to default Tailwind gray + red + the legacy 'primary' scale, drop the serif, and switch voice register, making the back-office feel like a different product. Secondary weak spots: amber-tinted favorites chip on the operators page, two different error styles (terra vs red), MountainSea used only on the homepage, hardcoded 'Inter' literal in leaflet CSS, an orphan DoubleRule decoration, and uncontrolled Wikipedia thumbnail styling. Coherence on the brand-facing surface alone is ~9/10; weighted across the whole product including admin/operator areas it lands at 7/10. Key files: c:\\Users\\emanu\\Desktop\\claude project\\mercati-v2\\tailwind.config.js, app\\globals.css, app\\page.tsx, app\\[marketSlug]\\page.tsx, app\\aderisci\\page.tsx, components\\decorations.tsx, components\\MarketsQuickFinder.tsx, components\\AdesioneForm.tsx, app\\[marketSlug]\\operators\\page.tsx, app\\admin\\markets\\page.tsx, app\\operator\\page.tsx."

painPoints.summary: IMercati has a polished editorial brand voice (cream/olive/ink palette, Fraunces serif, OliveSprig decorations) that is consistently applied on public-facing pages but collapses entirely in the authenticated areas (operator dashboard, OperatorCard component) where generic Tailwind defaults (white cards, gray-700 labels, primary-600 buttons, blue/pink social links) clash with the rest. There are at least four divergent "operator card" implementations and three "stat/feature card" patterns that should be unified into shared components. Common citizen journeys (Home → Operatore detail) require 4 clicks despite the rich nesting under [marketSlug]/c/[comuneSlug]. Every page uses `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` (44 files) with zero `loading.tsx` files and no skeletons — users see blank pages or inline "Caricamento…" text on every navigation. The market home page packs 7+ scroll sections (hero, finder, favorites, map, manifesto, zones, CTA, footer) onto a single screen, and the comune page nests a hero, sticky tabs, dettaglio card, map, operators list and prev/next nav inside an already-deep route. Admin uses native `confirm()` dialogs and `alert()` for destructive cascade operations, breaking visual continuity with the rest of the surface.

painPoints.title: Three+ competing 'operator card' implementations with conflicting styles
painPoints.location: components/OperatorCard.tsx:18-122 vs app/operatori/page.tsx:167-214 vs app/[marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx:213-252 vs app/operator/page.tsx:66-86
painPoints.description: OperatorCard.tsx uses white bg, rounded-lg, shadow-lg, gray-900 text, blue-100/pink-600 social pills, primary-600 button — pure Tailwind generic. The /operatori hub renders its own cream-50 / olive-500 card inline with a 40px photo header. The market-scoped /[marketSlug]/operators page renders a third variant (bg-white border, olive-300 hover, with a 'Vedi sulla mappa' pill). The /operator hub renders a fourth variant (bg-white shadow rounded-xl, primary-50 placeholder, gray-100 chip). None share a component. Maintaining/restyling operator presentation requires touching four files and reasoning about four different visual languages.

painPoints.title: Operator dashboard breaks the editorial brand voice entirely
painPoints.description: Public site is cream-100 / olive-500 / Fraunces serif with widest-plus tracking. The authenticated operator area switches to bg-white, text-gray-900, primary-100/primary-600 buttons, rounded-xl shadow, gray-300 inputs, generic 'Caricamento…' fallback. No OliveSprig, no serif headings, no cream palette. An operator who just clicked through a beautifully editorial site lands on what looks like a different product.

painPoints.title: Every page is force-dynamic with zero loading skeletons
painPoints.location: 44 files use 'export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"'; no app/**/loading.tsx exists
painPoints.description: Server pages opt out of caching entirely (force-dynamic on app/page.tsx:15, app/[marketSlug]/page.tsx:15, app/[marketSlug]/c/[comuneSlug]/page.tsx:12, etc.) but provide no Suspense boundaries or loading.tsx files. Client pages fall back to a plain text 'Caricamento…' (operator/page.tsx:43, operator/[id]/page.tsx:87, operatori/page.tsx:152) or one centered spinner (app/[marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx:182-186). Users on slower connections see a blank or jumpy white screen on every route change and no perceived-performance buffer for the map, photos, or operator lists.

painPoints.title: Market home page is a 7-section scroll wall (cognitive overload)
painPoints.description: Above-the-fold has: back-to-Provincia link, hero photo (4/5 aspect), title block, OliveSprig + comuni count, description, market_days, AND a 460px MarketViewer map. Then a 4-up 'shortcut' grid (Banchi/Calendario/Notizie/Meteo), then 'I borghi' grid of comune cards with photos, then 'Mercati di questa zona' table-like list, then prev/next zone navigation. Five competing visual hierarchies (hero, shortcut nav, photo grid, list, prev/next) on one route. Users opening the page from a search link are immediately bombarded.
impactedJourneys: Citizen checking 'what's at my zone this week'

painPoints.title: Common journey (Home → Operatore) takes 4 clicks minimum
painPoints.description: From the homepage the only direct paths to a specific operator are: (a) /operatori hub (1 click but no map context), or (b) Zone card → Comune card → ComuneSessionsExplorer tab → Operator link = 4 clicks, with the operator list only visible after picking the right session tab inside ComuneSessionsExplorer (components/ComuneSessionsExplorer.tsx:192-226). The 'Banchi' shortcut on the zone page (app/[marketSlug]/page.tsx:73) takes you to the operators list but loses the comune/session context that the user was navigating toward.
impactedJourneys: Citizen looking for 'my baker at the Tuesday Camporosso market'
Operator sharing their own profile URL
Discoverability of operators via map pins

painPoints.title: ComuneSessionsExplorer hides operators behind an extra tab interaction
painPoints.description: When a comune has multiple session days, the operators list is filtered by the selected tab (operatorsForSession = operators.filter(o => o.schedule_id === active.id), line 92-95). A user landing on /val-nervia/c/camporosso only sees the operators of the currently active tab, not all operators in the comune — with no affordance signaling that switching tabs reveals a different set of stalls. The empty-state copy 'Nessun operatore ancora registrato per questo mercato' (line 202) doesn't hint that they may exist under another tab.
impactedJourneys: Citizen searching for a specific seller
Tourist scanning what's available across the week

painPoints.title: Inconsistent button language: pill, rounded-full, rounded-md, rounded-sm in the same surface
painPoints.location: app/page.tsx:124-135 (rounded-full pills), app/[marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx:150-180 (rounded-full chips), app/operator/[id]/page.tsx:102-107 (rounded-md), components/OperatorCard.tsx:115 (rounded-md), app/admin/page.tsx:256-273 (rounded-sm)
painPoints.description: Public hero CTAs and chips use rounded-full ink/olive. Admin uses rounded-sm cream cards. Operator dashboard uses rounded-md primary-600. Within the same /[marketSlug]/operators page, the favorites toggle is rounded-full while operator cards are rounded-sm and 'Indicazioni' is rounded-full ink. There is no shared Button primitive — every page invents its own.

painPoints.title: Stat / ActionCard / FeatureCard duplicated inline across admin pages
painPoints.location: app/admin/page.tsx:235-275 (Stat + ActionCard locally defined), app/[marketSlug]/admin/page.tsx:57-93 (similar cards inline), app/[marketSlug]/page.tsx:149-167 (features shortcut also a card grid)
painPoints.description: The super-admin dashboard defines local Stat and ActionCard components. The market admin page reinvents nearly identical 'icon + title + desc + arrow' cards inline using the same olive/cream palette but with slightly different padding (p-5 vs p-4), different icon size (w-8 vs w-3.5), and different hover treatment. The zone home page renders yet another variant (the 4-up shortcut). Three almost-identical patterns, three implementations.

painPoints.title: Native browser confirm() and alert() in admin destructive flows
painPoints.location: app/admin/sessions/page.tsx:62-82 (toggleMarket and toggleSession use confirm() and alert())
painPoints.description: Cascade operations that turn off/on all sessions of a zone use the browser's confirm() dialog with a multi-line message ('Tutte le N sessioni... verranno SPENTE in cascata...') and report errors via alert(). This breaks the editorial visual continuity for the most consequential admin action and offers no styling, no undo, no consistent error surface. Users who manage many zones train themselves to dismiss the dialog quickly.
impactedJourneys: Bulk activation at the start of summer fairs

painPoints.title: Inconsistent hero treatment across page types
painPoints.location: app/page.tsx:101-139 (no photo, MountainSea decoration); app/[marketSlug]/page.tsx:82-145 (4/5 photo left + map right); app/[marketSlug]/c/[comuneSlug]/page.tsx:67-96 (smaller 220px photo left, no map in hero); app/operatori/page.tsx:94-111 (no photo, OliveSprig only); app/admin/page.tsx:83-89 (label + h1 + p, no decoration)
painPoints.description: Five page templates, five different hero treatments. The provincia hero has no image but a huge MountainSea SVG. The zone hero has a portrait photo plus a map. The comune hero has a smaller portrait photo and no map. The operatori hub has no photo. The admin dashboards have a compact label + heading. There's no shared Hero component; aspect ratios (5/4, 4/5, 3/2) and decoration density vary by page, making the site feel like a collection of microsites rather than one product.
impactedJourneys: First impressions across all entry points

painPoints.title: Inconsistent container widths and paddings cause visual jumps between routes
painPoints.location: app/page.tsx:141 (no max-w), app/[marketSlug]/page.tsx:84 (max-w-6xl) then :147 (max-w-5xl); app/[marketSlug]/c/[comuneSlug]/page.tsx:69 (max-w-6xl) then :98 (max-w-5xl); app/[marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx:127 (max-w-6xl); app/[marketSlug]/operators/[id]/page.tsx:50 (max-w-4xl); app/operator/[id]/page.tsx:90 (max-w-3xl); app/admin/page.tsx:82 (max-w-6xl)
painPoints.description: Six different max-width tokens across the app (no max-w, 6xl, 5xl, 4xl, 3xl, 2xl). Worse, within the same /[marketSlug]/page.tsx, the hero is max-w-6xl and the rest of the page is max-w-5xl, so content visibly shifts inward when scrolling past the hero. There's no Container primitive enforcing a system.
impactedJourneys: Visual continuity between hero and body sections

painPoints.title: Two parallel admin information architectures: per-market vs super-admin
painPoints.location: app/admin/* (super-admin: dashboard, sessions, markets, adesioni) and app/[marketSlug]/admin/* (zone admin: places, schedules, operators, news, events)
painPoints.description: Identical concerns (manage sessions, operators) live in two separate route trees with overlapping but non-identical menus. The super-admin dashboard at /admin lists 'Accendi/Spegni', 'Gestione zone', 'Adesioni'. The market-scoped admin at /[marketSlug]/admin lists 'Tutti i banchi', 'News', 'Eventi', plus a 'Gestione per luogo' section with per-place schedule rows. A user with both roles needs to learn two mental models. There is also no breadcrumb from /[marketSlug]/admin back to /admin.
impactedJourneys: Zone admin who is also super-admin

painPoints.title: OperatorCard.tsx component is defined but not used by the operators list
painPoints.location: components/OperatorCard.tsx (orphaned), Grep shows zero imports anywhere
painPoints.description: The OperatorCard component (with the most complete UI: payment icons, social links, languages, rating, navigation CTA) is not imported anywhere — Grep for 'OperatorCard' returns only the file itself. Meanwhile /[marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx, /operatori/page.tsx, and /operator/page.tsx each roll their own minimal card. The richer, designed component sits dead in the tree while the pages render less informative versions.
impactedJourneys: Citizen evaluating an operator (sees less info than designed)

painPoints.title: Schedule/session admin page mixes 4 unrelated tasks in one screen
painPoints.location: app/[marketSlug]/admin/s/[scheduleId]/page.tsx (state for: session info, place info with sibling schedules, area positions, operators list, create-operator form, link-existing-operator search)
painPoints.description: Per-session admin manages: editing session fields, drawing a polygon area (linked to place), listing operators on the session, creating a new operator inline (showCreate state), and searching/linking an existing cross-market operator (showLink state with searchQ/searchResults). Five tasks behind two modal toggles on one page, fetched through three different API endpoints (markets/by-slug, schedules/list, markets/{id}/places). Cognitive load for the zone admin is high and discoverability of features (link existing operator) depends on the user knowing to click 'Link2'.

painPoints.title: Empty / error states have no shared visual language
painPoints.location: app/[marketSlug]/operators/page.tsx:188-191 (red-50/red-200/red-800), components/ComuneSessionsExplorer.tsx:200-203 (cream-50 border), app/operatori/page.tsx:154-159 (cream-50 border), app/operator/page.tsx:44-51 (centered text-2xl bold), app/admin/page.tsx:133 ('Nessun dato ancora' as italic text)
painPoints.description: Error blocks use bright red Tailwind defaults; empty states alternate between cream-bordered boxes, plain italic text, and centered titles. No EmptyState / ErrorState primitive. The bright red error block in particular reads like a different application popped in.
impactedJourneys: Admin pages with no rows yet

journeys.goal: Voglio sapere dove c'è il mercato sabato vicino a me
currentSteps: Atterra su / (HomePage) — vede hero 'Mercati di terra e di mare' con CTA 'Trova un mercato'
Clicca 'Trova un mercato' (anchor #trova) oppure scorre fino alla sezione MarketsQuickFinder
Clicca il chip 'Sabato' (uno dei 7 Chip in MarketsQuickFinder)
Scorre la lista di max 24 card filtrate (sorted alfabeticamente per comune) cercando un comune che conosce — non c'è ordinamento per distanza geografica
Per capire se è 'vicino' deve aprire ciascuna card (Link verso /[marketSlug]/c/[comuneSlug]) per vedere la mappa, oppure tornare alla home e usare la UnifiedMap nella sezione 'Dove sono i mercati'
Sulla ComunePage vede ComuneSessionsExplorer con la mappa del singolo comune, e finalmente capisce dove andare
frictionPoints: Nessuna geolocalizzazione: 'vicino a me' non è una feature — il citizen deve sapere a memoria quali comuni sono vicini
Il chip 'Sabato' filtra correttamente ma la lista è ordinata alfabeticamente, non per prossimità (sort in MarketsQuickFinder.tsx riga 73-82)
La mappa principale (UnifiedMap) e il finder a chip sono due sezioni separate non sincronizzate: filtrare 'Sabato' nel finder non filtra i pin della mappa sotto

La sigla del giorno è scritta in stringa libera ('ogni Sabato', '1° sabato del mese') quindi il filtro 'Sabato' può includere mercati che NON ci sono questo sabato (logica weekdayOf in MarketsQuickFinder)
Tutti i 24+ risultati hanno la stessa gerarchia visiva: niente badge 'oggi' / 'questo weekend' / 'più vicino'
Per vedere distanza/percorso bisogna fare almeno 3 click (home → chip Sabato → apri card comune) e poi serve uscire dal sito verso Google Maps
idealSteps: Atterra su / — il sistema chiede permesso geolocalizzazione e mostra di default i 3 mercati di sabato più vicini (card con distanza in km e bottone 'Indicazioni')
Clicca direttamente la card del mercato preferito
Vede mappa + indicazioni stradali integrate (deep link a Google/Apple Maps con un solo tap)

journeys.goal: Sono in vacanza a Diano, c'è un mercato di prodotti tipici questa settimana?
currentSteps: Atterra su / e vede hero generico 'Provincia di Imperia' — non sa che 'Diano' corrisponde alla zona 'golfo-dianese'
Scorre alla sezione 'Un territorio, tappa per tappa' (lista zone editoriale) e cerca con gli occhi un nome che assomigli a 'Diano'
Trova la card 'Golfo Dianese' (con foto di Diano Marina come ZONE_HERO_COMUNE) e clicca → naviga a /golfo-dianese (MarketHomePage)
Sulla MarketHomePage vede l'hero zona, mappa e 4 feature link (Banchi, Calendario, Notizie, Meteo)

Per capire i 'prodotti tipici' deve cliccare /golfo-dianese/operators (lista banchi) oppure aprire una singola sessione /golfo-dianese/c/diano-marina per vedere la classifica per settori (settori field: alimentare/antiquariato/artigianato/varie)
Per sapere se c'è 'questa settimana' deve aprire /golfo-dianese/calendar oppure /calendar globale e cercare nelle prossime 7 giornate
In alternativa può usare il finder in homepage e digitare 'Diano' nella search — ma 5+ click totali sono necessari per arrivare alla risposta completa
frictionPoints: Il sito non rileva la lingua/origine del visitatore: niente segnale 'sei turista, eccoti i mercati tipici della settimana'
Il concetto 'prodotti tipici' non è un filtro pubblico: settori esiste nel DB (classifySchedule alimentare/antiquariato/artigianato/varie) ma la home non offre un chip 'Alimentare/Tipico'
'Questa settimana' non è un filtro nel finder: solo 'Oggi' e 'Weekend' come scorciatoie
La mappatura comune→zona è implicita (Diano Marina = golfo-dianese): un turista non sa che deve cliccare 'Golfo Dianese'
Nessuna pagina di tipo /diano-marina con sintesi 'tutto quello che succede qui questa settimana'
Le card zone in homepage mostrano foto bella ma niente indicazione 'specialità: ortofrutta, fiori, antiquariato'
idealSteps: Atterra su / e digita 'Diano' nel finder (o usa geolocalizzazione) — appare subito una card 'A Diano questa settimana'
Clicca la card e arriva su una pagina aggregata che lista le 2-3 sessioni dei prossimi 7 giorni filtrabili per 'Alimentare/Tipico'
Clicca il giorno desiderato e vede mappa + orari + foto dei banchi tipici

Vede schermata di conferma 'Richiesta ricevuta — risposta entro 48 ore' — il flusso pubblico finisce qui. Per accedere alla dashboard operatore (/operator) deve aspettare che un admin lo censisca e gli mandi credenziali via email (login a /login?next=/operator)
frictionPoints: Il copy spiega il 'perché' ma non il 'cosa succede dopo': niente roadmap visiva (passo 1 invio form → passo 2 ti chiamiamo → passo 3 scheda banco online)
Nessun esempio di scheda operatore da vedere prima di iscriversi: l'operatore non sa che riceverà /operator/[id] con foto, descrizione, prodotti — manca un 'vedi una scheda esempio'
Il form chiede 'mercati frequentati' come campo libero invece che multi-select sulle zone esistenti — operatore non sa se basta scrivere 'Diano' o serve essere precisi
Tempo stimato implicito ('Cinque minuti') ma 6 campi liberi sono troppi al primo touch — basterebbe nome+email+telefono+attività
Niente FAQ inline: 'è davvero gratis?', 'devo essere ambulante registrato?', 'posso togliere la scheda?'
Nessun riferimento al portale /operator esistente per chi è già stato censito — un operatore che torna sul sito non sa che esiste un'area riservata
idealSteps: Vede una pagina che spiega in 3 step grafici + un mini-form 3 campi (nome, telefono, mercato dove lavori da dropdown) + 'guarda un esempio di scheda' (link a /operator/demo)
Compila e invia — riceve subito email con riepilogo e prossimi passi, e un link per pre-caricare 1 foto del banco se vuole

journeys.goal: Devo spegnere temporaneamente le sessioni della prossima settimana per maltempo
currentSteps: Va su / (se non loggato) e naviga manualmente a /admin (non c'è link pubblico verso admin nella home/footer — middleware.ts protegge la rotta)
Se non loggato: redirect a /login → inserisce credenziali → torna su /admin (AdminRoot dashboard)
Sulla dashboard vede 5 stat + 3 ActionCard — clicca 'Accendi / Spegni' (ActionCard verso /admin/sessions)
Su /admin/sessions (AdminSessionsPage) vede in alto la griglia 'Zone aggregate' (toggle market-level con cascade) e sotto 'Sessioni singole' con filtri q/marketFilter/statusFilter
Non esiste filtro 'data': l'admin deve mentalmente identificare quali sessioni cadono nella prossima settimana guardando il campo `giorno` (stringa libera tipo 'ogni mercoledì')
Per ogni sessione da spegnere clicca il bottone Power → chiama PATCH /api/schedules/[id] con is_active=false — un click per sessione, no bulk action, no scheduling temporale

Per riaccenderle dopo la settimana di maltempo deve tornare e cliccare ON una per una (o spegnere/riaccendere la zona intera, ma il cascade riaccende anche sessioni che erano già OFF prima — il confirm avverte)
frictionPoints: Nessuna selezione multipla / bulk toggle: per spegnere 20 sessioni servono 20 click + 20 round-trip API
Nessun toggle 'temporaneo con data di riaccensione': il flag is_active è binario, l'admin deve ricordarsi di riaccendere manualmente lunedì prossimo
Il modello dati 'giorno' è una stringa libera ('ogni martedì', '1° sabato del mese') — non c'è il concetto di occurrence-per-data, quindi non si può spegnere 'solo il sabato 14 giugno'
Nessuna motivazione/log visibile per lo spegnimento: chi guarda il toggle OFF non sa se è maltempo, cancellazione, errore di import

Il cascade zona→sessioni è all-or-nothing: utile per spegnere una zona intera ma pericoloso perché riaccendendo riaccende anche sessioni che erano OFF per altri motivi (il confirm() avverte ma non offre 'salva stato precedente')
Nessuna comunicazione automatica verso il pubblico: spegnere una sessione la nasconde dalla mappa/calendario ma non genera banner 'mercato sospeso per maltempo'
Nessuna preview 'cosa vedrà il cittadino dopo': l'admin deve aprire /[marketSlug] in un'altra tab per verificare
idealSteps: Da /admin clicca 'Spegni per maltempo' (shortcut nuovo nella dashboard)
Seleziona range di date (lun-dom prossima settimana) + zone/sessioni con multi-select e motivo (preset 'Maltempo')
Conferma — il sistema applica is_active=false con data di riaccensione automatica e pubblica un banner 'Sospeso per maltempo dal X al Y' sulle pagine pubbliche interessate

proposal.northStar: IMercati feels like opening a slow Sunday magazine about Liguria. You land on a wide, almost cinematic photograph of crates of fava beans on stone, a single serif headline whispering "Imperia, mercato per mercato", and you scroll — not click — through the province. Each market is treated as a feature article: a hand-set wordmark, a dateline ("Ventimiglia, venerdì mattina, dal 1880"), a long paragraph of pure prose about who sells what and why, then quiet utility (orari, mappa, meteo) appearing only when the reader is ready. Type is generous Crimson Pro on cream #F4EEE2, with olive #5C6B47 used like a fountain-pen ink for marginalia. Photography is large, uncropped, never carouselled — one image breathes per fold. The interface barely interrupts; controls fade in with the scroll, calendar and map are inline figures with captions, and operators are introduced like profiled artisans, not listings. It feels read, not used — closer to Apartamento than to TripAdvisor — but every utilitarian answer (when, where, is it raining) is always one quiet sticky-rail click away.
principles: One image per fold. No carousels, no grids of thumbnails above the fold — a single full-bleed photograph carries the page, captioned in small caps like a magazine plate.
Prose before chrome. Every market, operator and zone leads with a written introduction (60-180 words of editorial copy from a real author byline) before any filter, button, or data table appears.

Serif headlines, sans utility. Crimson Pro for all narrative type; a single neutral sans (Inter) only for buttons, form fields, and tabular data — the typographic split signals 'reading mode' vs 'doing mode'.

Utility is a sticky margin, not a hero. Quick actions (oggi è aperto?, indicazioni, meteo) live in a thin right-rail or sticky footer strip that follows the scroll, so the article remains the main column.
Slow reveal over information density. Use scroll-triggered fade-ins, asymmetric two-column layouts, drop caps, pull quotes and end-marks. Nothing flashes, nothing badges, nothing pulses.

publicRoutes.purpose: The cover. Full-bleed seasonal photograph, masthead 'IMercati — una guida ai mercati della provincia di Imperia', editorial intro paragraph, and below the fold a vertical 'sommario' of the 5 zones rendered as numbered editorial cards (No. 01 Ventimiglia, No. 02 Sanremo...). No filter UI on the cover; the QuickFinder collapses into a sticky 'cerca il tuo mercato' rail that appears only after scrolling past the masthead.
publicRoutes.layoutSketch: Fold 1: 100vh photo + serif H1 + dateline. Fold 2: 600-word manifesto in a 65ch single column, drop cap, signed 'La redazione'. Fold 3: 'Il sommario' — 5 numbered articles vertically stacked, each = small caps issue number + photo half-width + title + 2-line dek. Fold 4: 'Questa settimana' — inline calendar strip rendered as a magazine sidebar. Footer: colophon (credits, contributors, sources).

publicRoutes.purpose: The feature article on one zone. Replaces both the current zone homepage AND the dead /search route. Each market gets its own typographic identity (one of 5 curated palette+ornament pairs) so Ventimiglia visually differs from Oneglia. Long-read structure with utility folded inline as figures.
publicRoutes.layoutSketch: Fold 1: full-bleed hero photo + 'N° 02' eyebrow + 'Sanremo' serif H1 + dek 'Il sabato di piazza Eroi Sanremesi, dal 1898'. Fold 2: 200-word lede in 60ch column, drop cap. Fold 3: 'Quando' — schedules as a typeset table (not a calendar widget) with footnotes for exceptions. Fold 4: 'Dove' — the map as a captioned full-bleed figure with handwritten-style pin labels. Fold 5: 'I banchi' — operators presented as a 2-column scrolling list of profiles (photo left, 80-word bio right), not cards. Fold 6: 'Da sapere' — meteo + parcheggi + avvisi merged into a single sidebar-style block. Sticky right rail throughout: 'apre tra 3 giorni · sabato 7:00'.

publicRoutes.purpose: Operator profile as a magazine personality piece. Replaces both /[marketSlug]/operators (list view becomes an inline section of zone page) and /[marketSlug]/operators/[id]. Slug-based for shareability and SEO.
publicRoutes.layoutSketch: Fold 1: portrait photograph 60vh + serif name H1 + eyebrow 'Banco N° 14 · Pesce fresco' + dek with years of activity. Fold 2: 150-word profile prose. Fold 3: 'Cosa trovi' — products as a typeset menu (not cards) with prices in small caps and Italian-style price markers. Fold 4: 'Quando lo trovi' — sessions as inline calligraphic timetable. Fold 5: 'Come pagare / lingue parlate' — pictograms with handwritten labels. Sidebar: contatti + social as a vCard-style block.

publicRoutes.purpose: The single unified calendar — merges /calendar and all /[marketSlug]/calendar. Editorial 'agenda della settimana' framing with zone filter as a magazine TOC, not chips.
publicRoutes.layoutSketch: Fold 1: serif H1 'L'agenda' + dek + this-week's editor's pick callout. Fold 2: week view as a magazine spread — 7 days as vertical columns, each appointment a tiny typeset block with market name in italics. Sidebar: zone filter as a TOC ('In questo numero: Ventimiglia · Sanremo · ...'), category filter as small caps tags.

publicRoutes.purpose: Province-wide operator directory — replaces /operatori. Framed as a 'rubrica' (column) titled 'I volti dei mercati'. Editorial featured operator at top, then alphabetical index.
publicRoutes.layoutSketch: Fold 1: 'Featured questa settimana' — one operator as a half-page profile. Fold 2: alphabetical index typeset like a magazine contributors page (A — Antonelli · Banco 12 · Sanremo / B — Bertolino · ...). Filters fold into a sticky thin top strip on scroll.

publicRoutes.purpose: Operator onboarding, reframed as 'Diventa un volto di IMercati' — letter-from-the-editor style invitation, then the form as a single-column reading-paced flow.
publicRoutes.layoutSketch: Fold 1: editorial letter (180 words, signed). Fold 2: form as a typeset questionnaire, one question per fold, large serif labels.

publicRoutes.purpose: Merges /privacy + /cookie + about + credits into a single editorial colophon page — magazine-style 'note di redazione'.

publicRoutes.purpose: Authentication, redesigned in the same cream/olive system. No more generic gray.

publicRoutes.purpose: Authenticated operator's 'la mia pagina' — dashboard reframed as 'la tua rubrica'. Auto-redirects if single stall owned.
publicRoutes.layoutSketch: List of owned banchi as editorial cards with last-edited timestamps and 'pubblicato / bozza' state.

publicRoutes.purpose: Self-edit of operator profile in the cream/olive system, with live preview pane showing how the magazine-style public page will read.
publicRoutes.layoutSketch: Split view: left = form fields with serif labels; right = sticky live preview rendered exactly like the public profile.

publicRoutes.purpose: Product CRUD as a typeset menu editor (not cards).

adminRoutes.purpose: Super-admin dashboard, renamed 'La redazione' — KPI cards become editorial pull-quote tiles ('Questa settimana: 3.214 lettori, 47 nuovi banchi, 2 zone in pausa'). Top zones/operators rendered as a leaderboard typeset like a magazine ranking. Recent adesioni as an inbox preview strip.

Gestione zone — CRUD markets list. Each market shown as an editorial card with on/off toggle (Accendi/Spegni con cascade), Excel tools button per row, and 'apri rubrica' link. New zone button as a serif 'Apri un nuovo numero' CTA.

Market edit + market admin assignment + Excel operators tools (export/template/import with dry-run preview) + place area drawer (MarketAreaDrawer per luogo, polygons shared across sessions) + sessions overview. Single long admin page with anchored TOC sidebar.
Per-session admin — banchi della sessione: create, link existing cross-market operator, on/off toggle, schedule binding.
Per-zone operators list with filters, ExcelOperatorsTools, create-new-operator with schedule binding.
Per-zone operator edit — profile, M:N presences (link operator to multiple sessions/markets), account invite email flow.
Per-zone news/avvisi CRUD. Global flag visible only to super-admin (publishes across all zones).
Per-zone events CRUD with category, date range, photo.
Super-admin all-sessions view (including inactive) — Accendi/Spegni toggle with cascade. Sortable table with zone column.

Adesioni operatori inbox (super-admin). Each adesione as an editorial 'lettera in arrivo' card with AdesioneStatoToggle (in-attesa/accettata/rifiutata) and PATCH/DELETE actions. Filter by stato + zona.

<Dateline /> — small-caps 'Sanremo · sabato mattina · dal 1898' marginalia line
<DropCap /> — first-letter drop cap for lede paragraphs
<PullQuote /> — large italic serif quote with author attribution
<EndMark /> — typographic ornament closing each article (◆)
<TypesetTable /> — schedules and products rendered as magazine tables, not data grids
<UtilityRail /> — sticky right-side thin column with 'apre tra X giorni', meteo glyph, jump-to anchors

brandMoves: Commission or curate one signature photograph per zone (golden-hour, uncropped, at least 2400px wide) and treat it as inviolable — never overlay UI on top, never crop into a thumbnail, always full-bleed with a captioned plate number.
Replace the generic stat cards on the admin dashboard with editorial pull-quotes: instead of 'Operatori attivi: 247' show 'Duecentoquarantasette banchi, undici comuni, una provincia. — La redazione'.

Introduce a typographic system: Crimson Pro for all narrative, Inter only for forms/data, and a small-caps tracking-wide treatment for all metadata (datelines, captions, byline). Ban ALL CAPS uppercase except where small-caps.

Give every zone its own identity: 5 curated palette accents (e.g. Ventimiglia = ochre, Sanremo = rose, Oneglia = sea-glass, Porto Maurizio = cobalt, Diano = terracotta) plus a unique ornamental glyph used as the end-mark for that zone's article.

Add real bylines: every zone article, every operator profile, every avviso signed by a named editor or contributor ('a cura di Giulia M.' / 'testo di Marco P.'). Builds editorial trust and humanizes a civic site.
Replace the 'Aderisci' CTA with a hand-set 'Diventa un volto di IMercati' invitation, framed as a letter from the editor — shifts onboarding from transactional to belonging.
Publish a weekly editor's pick ('La scelta della settimana') prominent on the cover and in the colophon RSS, turning the site into a return-visit ritual rather than a one-off lookup tool.
tradeoffs: Slower to find utilitarian answers: a tourist asking 'is the market open Saturday?' has to either find the sticky rail or scroll past prose — 1-2 extra seconds vs a chip grid.
Heavier page weight: full-bleed photography per zone means real CDN cost and slower LCP on 3G; mitigated by AVIF + careful sizes but never as cheap as utility-first cards.
Higher editorial burden: someone has to actually write 60-180 words per zone, per operator, per avviso. If copy isn't maintained the magazine feel collapses into 'lorem ipsum guida'.
Less screen real estate for filters: search/category filters are deprioritized into sticky rails, which can hurt power users (e.g. journalists looking for all 'pesce' vendors).
Calendar power-user features lose visibility: FullCalendar's month/week toggles become subtle, the magazine spread metaphor sacrifices some scannability for atmosphere.

Mobile is harder: editorial layouts shine on tablet/desktop but a magazine spread on a 375px iPhone risks becoming a tall narrow stack of photos and prose — needs careful mobile-specific typography rules.
Per-zone visual identity (5 palettes, 5 ornaments) increases QA surface and means every new zone added in the future needs a designer's touch, not just a slug + photo upload.

Accessibility complexity: drop caps, decorative ornaments, italic prose at small sizes all need explicit a11y treatment (aria-hidden ornaments, sufficient contrast on cream, screen-reader friendly datelines).
